arguments.task: Research Binance official developer/API documentation and first-party pages only where possible. Visit the Binance API docs site and official Binance developer/support/pricing pages. Extract evidence for: one-line product description, authentication methods for API access, whether access is self-serve or gated, API surface type (REST and/or GraphQL), breadth of the API, rate limit notes, and any official mention of MCP/server/GitHub registry. Also look for first-party docs on WebSocket/user data stream if relevant to breadth. Return concise findings with exact supporting URLs and quoted snippets.

Return structured bullet findings with quote snippets and exact source URLs only.

raw_result.description: Binance provides APIs and developer tools for accessing exchange market data, trading, account, and user-stream functionality across Binance products.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official docs distinguish public endpoints with no authentication from SIGNED/private endpoints that require an API key plus request signing; Binance supports HMAC, RSA, and Ed25519 key types/signatures in its developer docs.
raw_result.gating_reason: Public developer documentation is openly accessible, and API usage appears to be enabled through self-service account/API key creation rather than partner approval.

api_surface.breadth: Broad public developer surface documented across Spot and other Binance product areas, including REST API, WebSocket API/User Data Stream, and additional protocol/tooling references such as FIX and SBE in the official docs.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Official Binance developer docs include an SDKs & Tools section that mentions an MCP server for AI integration; exact repository/registry details were not fully verified in the gathered evidence.

raw_result.blocker: Core APIs are public and documented, but toolkit buildout likely needs manual handling for Binance-specific request signing and non-REST surfaces beyond the primary REST endpoints; MCP/repo details were not fully verified from the gathered evidence.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Official Spot REST docs include request-weight/IP limits and order-rate limits, and mention 429 responses for exceeding limits plus 418 bans for repeated violations. Exact numeric limits may vary by endpoint/product and were not fully normalized from the gathered evidence.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official docs show substantial API breadth beyond simple REST, including user data streams/WebSocket and references to FIX/SBE, plus SDKs/tools and an MCP-related tools page—strong signals of an active developer ecosystem.
raw_result.human_review_reason: The provided hint root URL returned a 404 while subpaths and the newer developers.binance.com portal appeared valid; access-tier evidence for account/API-key creation and exact MCP repository/registry details were inferred from gathered official pages but not exhaustively verified with a dedicated first-party account/pricing page.
